title: cylindrokelupha eberhardtii
intro: Cylindrokelupha eberhardtii, also known as the Cambodian dragon tree, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia that belongs to the family Phyllanthaceae. The medicinal properties of Cylindrokelupha eberhardtii are attributed to its leaves, roots, and bark, which contain bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. Consuming extracts from this plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and potential anti-cancer properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cylindrokelupha eberhardtii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, with the leaves and roots typically being used to make tea or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
